topic: The shift to learned: word embeddings
scope: The shift to learned representations for text (describe plainly; spine label not required).
key_concepts: The limitations of sparse representations (high dimensionality, orthogonal vectors for synonyms).
Dense vector representations (embeddings) map meaning to continuous space.
Similar meanings result in nearby vectors in the embedding space.
Embeddings are learned from large corpora based on word co-occurrence.

definitions.definition: Representasi vektor padat untuk sebuah kata, di mana kata dengan makna serupa memiliki nilai vektor yang berdekatan.
Vektor berdimensi rendah yang sebagian besar atau seluruh elemennya memiliki nilai bukan nol.

examples: Showing that 'mobil' and 'kucing' are perfectly orthogonal in BoW, but 'mobil' and 'kendaraan' will be close in embedding space.

relations.prerequisites: Understanding of sparse vectors from Ch 1 and s01.

exclusions: Math behind Word2Vec/GloVe training (Skip-gram, CBOW architectures).
